source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Doping changes the Fermi energy of a semiconductor. Consider silicon. At \( 300\text{K} \) the Fermi level of the pure material is nearly at the mid-point of the gap. Suppose that silicon is doped with donor atoms, each of which has a state \( 0.15\text{eV} \) below the bottom of the silicon conduction band, and suppose further that doping raises the Fermi level to \( 0.11\text{eV} \) below the bottom of that band. Calculate the probability that a state in the doped material (at the donor level) is occupied.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false suggest upstream metadata expects some image use
question_text does not explicitly reference a figure, diagram, or visual feature
all numerical quantities needed for solving appear directly in the text, so image reliance is not strongly evidenced
multi_step: solver must relate the donor level (0.15 eV below conduction band) to the Fermi level (0.11 eV below conduction band)
solver then applies an occupancy probability formula at 300 K using the energy difference
this is more than direct lookup, but still a short physics calculation rather than a long reasoning chain
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a specific value: 0.824
question provides explicit numerical inputs, enabling independent recomputation
multiple-choice options are provided, which supports answer cross-checking
asset_registry_record shows registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true
